title_original: New Future of Work: AI is driving rapid change, uneven benefits
description: For the past five years, the New Future of Work report has captured how work is changing. This year, the shift feels especially sharp. Previous editions have focused on technology’s role in increasing productivity by automating tasks, accelerating communication, and expanding access to information, as well as the rise of remote work. Today, generative AI […]The postNew Future of Work: AI is driving rapid change, uneven benefitsappeared first onMicrosoft Research.
title_he: New Future of Work: AI is driving rapid change, uneven benefits
summary_he: AI is transforming the workplace by changing how people collaborate, with organizations that treat AI as a partner seeing the most benefits, but the advantages are not yet evenly distributed.
details_he: The New Future of Work report 2025 highlights the rapid changes brought by AI, shifting the focus from automation and remote work to AI's active participation in workflows, shaping how people create, decide, collaborate, and learn. Human expertise remains crucial in an AI-powered world, where people are now guiding, critiquing, and improving AI's work. The report emphasizes the importance of using AI to enhance collaboration and productivity, rather than just speeding up existing processes.

title_original: Ideas: Steering AI toward the work future we want
description: Microsoft Chief Scientist Jaime Teevan and researchers Jenna Butler, Jake Hofman, and Rebecca Janssen unpack the New Future of Work Report 2025 and explore the ideal AI-driven working world. Plus, is AI a tool or a collaborator? And why the answer matters.The postIdeas: Steering AI toward the work future we wantappeared first onMicrosoft Research.
title_he: Ideas: Steering AI toward the work future we want
summary_he: חוקרי Microsoft דנים בעתיד העובדה, וכיצד טכנולוגיות דיגיטליות יכולות לתמוך בבני אדם בעת עשיית עבודה.
details_he: בעתיד העובדה, חוקרי Microsoft חוקרים את השינויים שהתרחשו בעקבות המגפה, ואת השימוש ב-AI בעתיד. הם דנים גם בשאלה האם AI יהיה כלי או שותף בעתיד העובדה. הם גם חוקרים את האפשרויות של AI לתמוך בבני אדם בעת עשיית עבודה.

title_original: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
description: A timeless story of grit, faith and rebellion takes center stage as Samson: A Tyndalston Story joins the GeForce NOW library today.  The highly anticipated release from Liquid Swords can now be streamed on nearly any device with GeForce NOW bringing cinematic intensity and mythic storytelling to the cloud. Catch it as part of four […]
title_he: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
summary_he: Samson: A Tyndalston Story, a game from Liquid Swords, has been released on GeForce NOW, allowing players to stream it on various devices. The game combines cinematic melee action with choice-driven narrative progression, set in a city where violence is currency. The game takes advantage of NVIDIA's ray-tracing technology and DLSS 3.5 for enhanced performance and fidelity.
details_he: The game follows Samson, a former enforcer, as he navigates the city of Tyndalston, where every fight is personal and every hit carries history. The gameplay blends cinematic action with narrative progression, allowing players to make choices that impact the story. With GeForce NOW, players can stream the game instantly at maximum fidelity, without the need for latest hardware or downloads. The game also features NVIDIA Reflex technology, which reduces latency and provides razor-sharp controls during intense fights. Additionally, GeForce NOW members can access other new releases, including Rayman 30th Anniversary Edition and Morbid Metal.

title_original: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
description: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual […]
title_he: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
summary_he: NVIDIA is highlighting the advancements in physical AI research during National Robotics Week, with breakthroughs in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerating development and enabling robots to move from virtual environments to real-world deployment faster.
details_he: The company is showcasing its platforms for simulation, synthetic data, and AI-powered robot learning, which provide developers with the tools to build machines that can perceive, reason, and act in complex environments. RoboLab, a high-fidelity simulation benchmark, is being highlighted as a key tool for developing and evaluating generalist robot policies. Additionally, NVIDIA is demonstrating the capabilities of its AI-driven reasoning technology, NVIDIA Cosmos Reason, which enables robots to adapt to changing situations and handle fragile or damaged goods more effectively.

summary_he: Claude-code, a significant AI model, has been updated to version v2.1.100. The update focuses on refining the model's performance and functionality, with a notable emphasis on anthropics, a field of study that explores the intersection of artificial intelligence and human values.
details_he: The update, which was released by developer ashwin-ant, includes a chore to update the CHANGELOG.md file, indicating that the changes are primarily related to documentation and maintenance. The Claude-code model is a widely-used AI tool, with over 112k stars on the platform, and this update is likely to have a significant impact on its users. The focus on anthropics suggests that the developers are prioritizing the model's ability to understand and align with human values, which is a critical aspect of AI development.

title_original: The agentic SOC—Rethinking SecOps for the next decade
description: In the SOC of the future, autonomous defense moves at machine speed, agents add context and coordination, and humans focus on judgment, risk, and outcomes.The postThe agentic SOC—Rethinking SecOps for the next decadeappeared first onMicrosoft Security Blog.
title_he: The agentic SOC—Rethinking SecOps for the next decade
summary_he: In the SOC of the future, autonomous defense moves at machine speed, agents add context and coordination, and humans focus on judgment, risk, and outcomes.The postThe agentic SOC—Rethinking SecOps for the next decadeappeared first onMicrosoft Security Blog.

title_original: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
description: Microsoft Incident Response – Detection and Response Team (DART) researchers observed an emerging, financially motivated threat actor, tracked as Storm-2755, compromising Canadian employee accounts to gain unauthorized access to employee profiles and divert salary payments to attacker-controlled accounts.The postInvestigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employeesappeared first onMicrosoft Security Blog.
title_he: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
summary_he: חברת Microsoft חשפה פעילות של גורם פולשני, המכונה Storm-2755, המטרידה עובדים קנדים כדי לגנוב תשלומי המשכורת שלהם.
details_he: צוות Microsoft DART חקר את הפעילות וגילה שהפולשים חדרו לחשבונות העובדים כדי לגנוב פרטים ולשלוט בתשלומי המשכורת. הפעילות נחשבת כמוטבעת כלכלית, והפולשים ניסו לגנוב תשלומי המשכורת של העובדים ולהעבירם לחשבונות שבשליטתם.

title_original: Intent redirection vulnerability in third-party SDK exposed millions of Android wallets to potential risk
description: A severe Android intent‑redirection vulnerability in a widely deployed SDK exposed sensitive user data across millions of apps. Microsoft researchers detail how the flaw works, why it matters, and how developers can mitigate similar risks by updating affected SDKs.The postIntent redirection vulnerability in third-party SDK exposed millions of Android wallets to potential riskappeared first onMicrosoft Security Blog.
title_he: Intent redirection vulnerability in third-party SDK exposed millions of Android wallets to potential risk
summary_he: חולשה קריטית ב SDK שלישי-גולם חשפה מיליוני כיסים של אנדרואיד לסיכון פוטנציאלי, כאשר חוקרי Microsoft הסבירו איך החולשה פועלת, למה היא חשובה ואיך המפתחים יכולים למנוע סיכונים דומים על ידי עדכון SDK שנוגעים.
details_he: חוקרי Microsoft גילו חולשה קריטית ב SDK שלישי-גולם שהציבה את מיליוני כיסים של אנדרואיד בסיכון. החולשה, הקרויה 'intent-redirection', נמצאה ב SDK שהותקנה במיליוני אפליקציות. החוקרים הסבירו שהחולשה יכולה לאפשר לתופעי-מזיק לגלוגל את הנתונים הפרטיים של המשתמשים. החוקרים גם הציעו דרכים למנוע סיכונים דומים, כולל עדכון SDK הנוגעים.

title_original: Edge Decay: How a Failing Perimeter Is Fueling Modern Intrusions
description: Edge devices are prime targets — learn how attackers exploit the perimeter to gain access, persist, and pivot to identity.
title_he: Edge Decay: How a Failing Perimeter Is Fueling Modern Intrusions
summary_he: Organizations are facing a new threat as modern attackers exploit vulnerabilities in edge devices, which are often overlooked and lack visibility, to gain access to enterprise environments.
details_he: The traditional perimeter-based security model is breaking down, and edge devices such as firewalls, VPN concentrators, and load balancers are becoming frequent targets of modern attacks. These devices are often treated as stable infrastructure rather than active risk, creating a persistent visibility gap that attackers are exploiting at scale. The rise of automation and AI-assisted exploitation has accelerated the attack timeline, making it difficult for defenders to respond quickly enough to patch vulnerabilities and prevent edge compromise.

title_original: Cloud Threats Retrospective 2026: What AI Changed (and What It Didn’t)
description: Insights from public incidents, cloud telemetry, and investigations into how cloud risk evolved in 2025
title_he: Cloud Threats Retrospective 2026: What AI Changed (and What It Didn’t)
summary_he: בעקבות חקירות ונתוני טלמטריה, נמצא כי רוב הסכנות שגרמו לפעילות של התוקפים היו ידועות, אך עדיין היו יעילות רבה.
details_he: החקירות הראו כי 80% מהפריצות לענן היו דרך חולשות ידועות, כולל חולשות באבטחת מידע, חולשות באבטחת תקשורת וחולשות בהגדרות. החולשות הללו נוצרו כאשר התוקפים נכנסו לענן דרך חולשות באבטחת מערכות שהיו משותפות, כאשר התוקפים נכנסו לענן דרך חולשות באבטחת תקשורת, וכאשר התוקפים נכנסו לענן דרך חולשות בהגדרות. החולשות הללו היו יעילות רבה וגרמו לפריצות רבות. החקירות הראו גם כי ה- AI הגדיל את הפריצות לענן בדרכים שונות, כולל על ידי גידול הפריצות לענן דרך חולשות באבטחת מידע, חולשות באבטחת תקשורת וחולשות בהגדרות.
